search_queries_used: site:college-optometrists.org corneal abrasion symptoms assessment referral
site:college-optometrists.org annex 4 eye trauma visual loss pain referral
candidate_sources_rejected: eye drops, irrigation, removal, antibiotics and referral instructions
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The broad workflow includes blunt, penetrating, chemical, foreign-body and adnexal injury.
No medicine, procedure, irrigation or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children with possible eye injury; exact support is strongest for corneal abrasion and urgent anterior-eye presentations.
setting_applicability: Urgent eye assessment and referral principles partially transfer to emergency documentation.
UAE_applicability: Clinical warning features transfer, but UAE ophthalmology, chemical-exposure, trauma, safeguarding and referral pathways govern.
recency_verification: Current College clinical guidance version 1.5 (2025) and referral annex were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: Current publisher pages were used; no newer replacement was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact corneal-abrasion features section supports onset, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, risk context and injury mechanism for a possible superficial corneal injury; it does not diagnose an abrasion or cover every eye injury.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports minor-injury, foreign-body and contact-lens context plus pain, blepharospasm, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact assessment and referral section supports recording focused examination, appearance, time-critical features and specialist discussion or escalation only when assessed or clinician-decided.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of the involved area, fluorescein documentation, and referral when injury is deeper, contaminated, or possibly infected; medicine details are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact anterior-eye and trauma referral table supports documenting traumatic mechanism, urgent features and arranged handover only when identified and acted on by the clinician.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Vision loss, severe pain and other urgent eye presentations were reviewed as clinician assessment prompts, never as conclusions.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.

unresolved_source_gaps: Visual acuity details, laterality, contact lens, chemical exposure, penetrating mechanism, globe integrity, pupil, fluorescein, intraocular pressure, eyelid injury, diagnosis, treatment, procedure and exact referral urgency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
